item.title: Consolidate three merge strategies into parameterized flow
item.intent: The merge/squash/no-merge paths in _handle_sprint_success share one code flow with a branch only on the merge command itself. Saves ~40 lines of duplicated checkout/push/pull logic.
item.description: _handle_sprint_success has three near-identical code blocks for merge, squash, and no-merge. The remote sync block (push + pull + warning) is duplicated verbatim between squash (lines 2132-2138) and no-ff (lines 2162-2168). The merge_msg if/else (lines 2146-2150) is collapsible to one expression. Safety-commit message pattern is also repeated. Parameterize into: (1) no-merge early return with push and optional PR, (2) single merge path that branches only on the git merge command, (3) shared _sync_remote helper for push+pull.
steps: Extract _sync_remote helper: git push + git pull with warnings — used by both merge paths
Collapse merge_msg if/else to: merge_msg=merge: ITEM_ID dollar{merge_summary:-sprint branch}
Parameterize merge: single path that runs git merge --no-ff or git merge --squash based on MERGE_STRATEGY
Handle squash-specific explicit commit after merge --squash
Keep no-merge as early return with push and optional PR creation
Verify: all three strategies produce identical git history as before
acceptanceCriteria: A _sync_remote helper exists and is called by both merge paths
No duplicated push/pull/cleanup code between merge strategies
merge_msg construction is a single expression, not an if/else block
no-merge flag pushes branch and skips merge
